info.name: Serensia
info.description:
This API is in [alpha](#section/Stability/Alpha) stage, breaking changes are to be expected as we continue to improve and expand its features.

# Introduction

The Serensia API is documented in **OpenAPI Format** (former Swagger file format).

# URls

Base URL for APIs: if Serensia Intelligent Gateway is on https://test.exemple.com/

- API Base URL: https://test.exemple.com/api/pdp/v2
- Open API File (swagger) : https://test.exemple.com/api/pdp/v2/docs/pdp.v2.openapi.json
- Documentation : https://test.exemple.com/api/pdp/v2/docs

# Stability

Stability of each individual API endpoint is defined using the following terms:

## Alpha

An alpha component undergoes rapid iteration with a known set of users who must be tolerant of change. The number of users should be a curated, manageable set,
such that it is feasible to communicate with all of them individually.

Breaking changes must be both allowed and expected in alpha components, and users must have no expectation of stability.

## Beta

A beta component must be considered complete and ready to be declared stable, subject to public testing. Beta components should be exposed to an unknown and
potentially large set of users.

Because users of beta components tend to have a lower tolerance of change, beta components should be as stable as possible; however, the beta component must be
permitted to change over time. These changes should be minimal but may include backwards-incompatible changes to beta components. Backwards-incompatible changes
must be made only after a reasonable deprecation period to provide users with an opportunity to migrate their code. This deprecation period must be defined at
the time of being marked beta.

Beta components should be time-boxed and promoted to stable if no issues are found in the specified timeframe, which should be specified at the time of being
marked beta. A reasonable time period may vary, but a good rule of thumb is 90 days.

## Stable

A stable component must be fully-supported over the lifetime of the major API version. Because users expect such stability from components marked stable, there
must be no breaking changes to these components, subject to the caveats described below.

### Major versions

When breaking changes become necessary, the API producer should create the next major version of the API, and start a deprecation clock on the existing version.

Turn-down of any version containing stable components must have a formal process defined at the time of being marked stable. This process must specify a
deprecation period for users which provides them with reasonable advance warning.

### Isolated changes

On very rare occasions, it could be preferable to make a small, isolated breaking change, if this will only cause inconvenience to a small subset of users. (
Creating a new major version is an inconvenience to all users.) In this case, the API producer may deprecate the component, but must continue to support the
component for the normal turndown period for a stable component.

Important: Making an in-place breaking change in a stable API is considered an extreme course of action, and should be treated with equal or greater gravity as
creating a new major version.

### Emergency changes

In certain exceptional cases, such as security concerns or regulatory requirements, any API component may be changed in a breaking manner regardless of its
stability level, and a deprecation is not promised in these situations.

# Versions

This API follows [semantic versioning](https://semver.org/). The version number in the URL is the MAJOR. Any breaking change **will** lead to an increase of the
MAJOR. Breaking changes are changes that can potentially break an integration, it includes:

- removing or renaming an operation
- removing or renaming a parameter
- removing or renaming a response field
- adding a new required parameter
- making a previously optional parameter required
- changing the type of a parameter or response field
- removing enum values
- adding a new validation rule to an existing parameter
- changing authentication or authorization requirements

Any additive (non-breaking) changes will be available in the latest supported MAJOR API version, with a new Minor API version.

Additive changes are changes that should not break an integration. Additive changes include:

- adding an operation
- adding an optional parameter
- adding an optional request header
- adding a response field
- adding a response header
- adding enum values

Please contact support@serensia.com to register to the mailing list to be informed of API changes.

# Changelog

Version 1.0 is not officially released yet.
The changelog will be updated starting with the official release of 1.0 version.

# Authentication

## OpenID Bearer Token (OAuth2)

Serensia API is authenticated with a bearer token. The bearer token can be obtained with OpenID (client credentials flow) at https://[server]/openid.

Ask Serensia for your client_id and client_secret.

### Obtain the token from serensia open id server

OpenID (based on Oauth 2) is a standard. You can find the documentation for the client credentials grant
at: https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/access-tokens/client-credentials/

Example with CURL:

**Token query**

    curl --location 'http://your_server/openid/connect/token' \
    --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
    --data-urlencode 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
    --data-urlencode 'client_id=your_client_id' \
    --data-urlencode 'client_secret=your_client_secret'

**Token response**

    {
      "access_token": "your_token_value",
      "token_type": "Bearer",
      "expires_in": 3599
    }

### Using the token in API requests

You can use the token you obtained by adding an Authorization header of type Bearer on your api requests:
``
Authorization: Bearer your_token_value
``

Example with CURL

    curl --location 'http://your_server/api/v2/mail-reception/mailboxes' \
    --header 'Authorization: Bearer your_token_value'

# Multitenancy

The PDP is a multitenant system, it means that a single PDP instance serves multiple organizations (tenants), while keeping their data and configurations
isolated.

To ensure a strict isolation of data, most operations will be executed in the context of one specific tenant, and will only have access to the data of that
specific tenant.
The tenant to use is one of the route parameters of the endpoint.\
For example, `GET /pdp/members/{memberId}/invoices` will only query the invoices of the tenant that corresponds to the member with id `memberId`.

The list of accessible tenants is returned by the [Search tenants](#tag/Tenants/operation/Tenants_SearchTenants) endpoint.

# Rate Limiting

Rate Limiting is not yet implemented

# Status codes

All API endpoints provide standard HTTP Status Codes. The common codes are described below.

## 200 OK

The request succeeded and a response body is provided.

## 204 NoContent

The request succeeded.

## 400 BadRequest

Error: The provided request is invalid.

## 401 Unauthorized

Error: No valid authentication was provided.

## 403 Forbidden

Error: Provided authentication did not permit access to the resource.

## 404 Not Found

Error: The resource for URI was not found.

## 429 Too Many Requests

Error: The API client made too many calls : see Rate Limiting.

## 5xx Errors

Error: An internal error occured in Serensia Intelligent Gateway while processing the request. Please contact support@serensia.com with a copy of the request
and the resulting error response.

# Search operations

## Full text query

Search operations may provide a `fullText` filter in the form of a query parameter. This `fullText` filter allows the API consumers to perform a search over
multiple properties of the resource.\
For example, one may want to use the `fullText` filter to search invoices by reference:

``
GET /v2/pdp/e-invoicing/invoices?fullText=FAC01234
``

or even by reference prefix with the use of the wildcard (*):

``
GET /v2/pdp/e-invoicing/invoices?fullText=FAC0*
``

# Webhooks

Webhooks allow partners to be notified when specific events occur.

Webhooks work as follow:

- the partner chooses the specific event it wants to subscribe to, and subscribes to this specific event
- each time this specific event happens, a notification is pushed to the callback URI provided for this specific event

See below to get more details about the subscriptions and the notifications.

## Subscription

### Events

See [Events](#tag/Events) to get the list of all existing events that can be subscribed to.

### Subscription request

Subscription to an event can be achieved with [Create a subscription](#tag/Subscriptions/operation/WebhookSubscriptions_CreateWebhookSubscription).

When subscribing to a specific event identified with its `eventName` (say `pdp:invoice:status_updated`):

- a `callbackUri` (say `https://callback`)
- and a `callbackValidationToken` (say `clientToken`)

are provided as part of the request.

__Remark: the provided callback URI must use HTTPS (no HTTP callback URI will be allowed).__

### Callback validation request

As a response to this subscription request, an initial request will be made to this callback URI in order to validate it, as follow:

``
GET https://callback?validationToken=clientToken&challengeToken=randomChallengeToken
``

Note the `validationToken` and `challengeToken` that are provided as query parameters of the GET request.

The subscriber must respond to this request with a 200 OK response with the following JSON payload including the provided `challengeToken`:

```http request
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json

{ "challengeToken": "randomChallengeToken" }
```

If the callback URI does not respond as expected within an acceptable range of time, the subscription will not be created.

It is the responsibility of the subscriber to check that the `callbackValidationToken` provided in the subscription request is the same as
the `validationToken` the one sent back in the callback validation request.

### Subscription response

The `subscriptionId` along with a generated `secret` (a randomly generated UUID) will be provided as part of the response.
The `secret` is used to sign notification requests.

## Notifications

Each time the specific event happens, a notification will be pushed to the provided callback URI, as follow:

```http request
POST https://callback HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
Content-Type: application/json
Webhook-Signature: sha256=8634996b8c3d60bf26fc79b8292a4b10bf694e0a1497c808c49207a27c335296

jsonContent
```

### Payload

`jsonContent` is the JSON payload:

    {
      "timestamp": 1697177202.12345,
      "eventName": "pdp:invoice:status_updated",
      "eventData": { ... data specific to this event ... },
      "subscriptionId": 25,
      "tenantId": 34
    }

The defined [Events](#tag/Events) give the shape of the payload, with the `eventData` specific to each event.

### Signature

Notifications pushed to the `callbackUri` are signed using the `Webhook-Signature` header:

``
Webhook-Signature: sha256=8634996b8c3d60bf26fc79b8292a4b10bf694e0a1497c808c49207a27c335296
``

To verify the authenticity of the request, partners are invited to check the signature of the payload.

For example, if the payload is `jsonContent` and the `secret` is `dummySecret`, the corresponding hmac-sha256 signature is:

``
8634996b8c3d60bf26fc79b8292a4b10bf694e0a1497c808c49207a27c335296
``

In JavaScript, this could be achieved using the lib [js-sha256](https://github.com/emn178/js-sha256) and the following code:

```javascript
const sha256 = require("js-sha256")
sha256.hmac('dummySecret', 'jsonContent')
```

In C#, this could be achieved with:

```csharp
using HMACSHA256 hasher = new HMACSHA256(Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes("dummySecret"));
byte[] data = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes("jsonContent");
byte[] signatureBytes = hasher.ComputeHash(data);
string signatureHeaderValue = BitConverter.ToString(signatureBytes).Replace("-", "").ToLower();
```

**Note**: To ensure the hash matches the one sent in the request header, the jsonContent must exactly replicate the raw JSON payload used in the body of the
POST request
(including field order, formatting, and whitespace).

### Expected response

The callback URI must respond to notifications with a 200 OK status code to indicate that the notification has been successfully received by the partner.

The callback URI must respond as expected within an acceptable range of time.

### Considerations about notifications

#### Ordering

Serensia does not guarantee any ordering about event notifications that are pushed to partners.

As an example, it may be possible that an event A happens before an event B,
but the partner receives the notification of event B before the notification of event A.

Therefore, partners SHOULD NOT rely on the order in which they receive the notifications in order to process them.

#### Number of notifications

Serensia does not guarantee how many notifications are pushed for a specific event that happened.

As an example, it may be possible that an event A happens and the partner receives the notification of event A twice (or more).

Therefore, partners SHOULD consider that a notification for an event has already been sent / received.
Partners may consider to use the timestamp sent as part of the notification payload to uniquely identify event notifications.

request.description:
Retrieve a file representing the invoice with the given identifier. 
Formats:
- Original: get the file that has been received by the PDP for the invoice, in whatever format it has been received.
- CommercialPdf: get the PDF that was provided in the source file, if any, e.g. if the source file is a FacturX,
an UBL containing the PDF as attachment, or a PDF itself. If no PDF has been provided, a standard one will be generated instead.
- StandardPdf: get a PDF generated by SERENSIA that contains all the information about the invoice, and is compliant with the regulation. 
- FacturX: get the original file if it is a FacturX, or a generated one
- UblXml: get the original file if it is an XML UBL EN16931, or a generated one
- CiiXml: get the original file if it is an XML CII D16B EN16931, or a generated one
